item.title: Hiroshima University team meets DU VC
item.description: A three-member delegation, led by Md Jiaur Rahman, a researcher of Hiroshima University in Japan, called on Dhaka University vice-chancellor Professor ABM Obaidul Islam at the latter’s office on the campus in the capital on Monday....

item.title: Fundamentalist forces rise after July uprising: Serajul Islam Chy
item.description: Dhaka University Professor Emeritus Serajul Islam Choudhury on Tuesday said that the fundamentalist political forces including Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami had emerged in the country following the 2024 July Uprising....

item.title: IUB, Norwegian embassy ink deal on literature
item.description: Independent University, Bangladesh and the Royal Norwegian embassy in Dhaka signed an agreement to support the ‘Henrik Ibsen and Jon Fosse Festival in Bangladesh’, an initiative aimed at promoting Norwegian literature, culture, and creative expression among Bangladeshi audiences....

item.title: Anindita Oni’s womanhood on stone ends
item.description: Young visual artist Anindita Oni depicted womanhood in acrylic on stone in her first solo art exhibition titled She in Stone, which concluded on Tuesday at Daagi Art Garage at Hazaribagh in the capital....

item.title: 8 unproductive areas get 13pc of budget: rights campaigners
item.description: Rights campaigners said on Tuesday that Tk 1.23 lakh crore, or about 13 per cent of the proposed budget for the 2026–27 financial year, would be spent on eight unproductive areas....

item.title: IUB student killed in road crash days before Australia flight
item.description: A university student who was preparing to leave for Australia for higher education has lost his life in a road accident in Dhaka’s Fakirapool area early Tuesday just days before his scheduled departure....

item.title: Safiuddin remembered at BSA
item.description: Bangladesh Shilpakala Academy’s fine arts department organised a commemorative discussion on Safiuddin Ahmed, marking his 104th birth anniversary at its Chitrashala Auditorium in Dhaka on Tuesday....

item.title: Bangladesh aims for $1b yearly from carbon credits: report
item.description: Bangladesh can earn nearly $1 billion annually from the sale of carbon credits to be generated through the nationwide 25 crore tree plantation programme announced by the incumbent government, according to a government report....

item.title: Madrassah teacher arrested on rape charges in Lalmonirhat
item.description: The police on Tuesday arrested a madrassah teacher on charges of raping a housewife in Lalmonirhat’s Aditmari....

item.title: Two ASIs removed after suspect escapes Savar police camp
item.description: Two assistant sub-inspectors have been withdrawn from duty after a suspect in a student murder case linked to the 2024 mass uprising escaped from a police camp in Savar, police said on Tuesday....

item.title: Driver killed as truck falls into canal after bridge collapse
item.description: A driver was killed and his assistant was injured after a sand-laden truck fell into a canal following a bridge collapse on Tuesday in Barishal....

item.title: Banks’ investments in govt securities surge
item.description: Banks increased their investment in government securities by 35.5 per cent in 2025 as rising default loans, deteriorating asset quality and liquidity stress pushed many banks away from traditional lending activities......

item.title: Banks allowed to send consumer loans directly to vendors
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has permitted banks to disburse consumer loans directly to vendors through electronic fund transfers, replacing the mandatory use of pay orders and cheques that had been in place since 2004......

item.title: IMF opposes return of S Alam syndicate
item.description: The International Monetary Fund has sought a time-bound action plan for comprehensive banking sector reforms, specifically raising structural objections over the newly added Section 18(A) of the Bank Company Act, finance ministry sources said......

item.title: BSEC chair’s salary hiked to Tk 6 lakh a month
item.description: The government has increased the salary and allowances of the chairman of the Bangladesh Securities and Exchange Commission more than threefold and substantially increased the remuneration of commissioners as part of efforts to attract competent professionals to the capital market regulator....

item.title: BB facilitates NRBs with easy money repatriation
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has introduced a new account facility, the Non-Resident Convertible Taka Account (NRCTA), aimed at encouraging greater participation of Non-Resident Bangladeshis (NRBs) in the country’s economy by providing a flexible and fully repatriable investment and savings platform......

item.title: Foreign loan, grants to Bangladesh fall 23pc
item.description: Country’s foreign aid commitments suffered a decline of around 23 per cent compared with the corresponding period of the previous fiscal year, according to provisional data from the Economic Relations Division......

item.title: PKSF raises FY27 financing target by 45pc
item.description: Palli Karma-Sahayak Foundation has approved a financing target of Taka 160 billion for the fiscal year 2026-27, marking a 45.34 per cent increase from the outgoing fiscal year, with a strong focus on skills development, employment generation and sustainable economic growth for low-income people......

item.title: ‘Created in Bangladesh’ brand to spearhead creative economy
item.description: Bangladesh is set to launch a new national brand titled ‘Created in Bangladesh’ as part of a comprehensive strategy to transform the creative industry into a major source of employment, entrepreneurship, economic growth and national branding......

item.title: Mia Shaheb’s Maidan Khanqah to observe holy Ashura
item.description: Holy Ashura and the 1347th death anniversary of Hazrat Imam Hussain (RA) will be observed on Thursday at the Mia Shaheb’s Maidan Khankah Sharif at Luxmibazar in Dhaka. ...

item.title: Neymar edges towards Brazil return
item.description: Neymar could make his first appearance at this year’s World Cup in Brazil’s match against Scotland on Wednesday, with ‌Carlo Ancelotti weighing whether the forward is fit enough to return after a calf injury that has kept him out of the finals so far....

item.title: EU bets on digital euro to cut US tech addiction
item.description: The EU believes a digital euro is the answer to cutting its addiction to US payment systems like Visa and Mastercard as well as Apple Pay and Google Pay as the bloc seeks to favour European firms over others......

item.title: UK and markets await Burnham’s economic plans
item.description: Andy Burnham, expected to become Britain’s next prime minister after Keir Starmer resigned, has set out to reassure investors concerned that state spending and debt could rise under his leadership......

item.title: Ultra-reclusive Turkmenistan slowly opens up to tourists
item.description: From a gas crater dubbed the ‘Gateway to Hell’ to the shimmering marble buildings of its capital, Turkmenistan is tentatively opening up to tourists after decades of isolation......

item.title: Won’t allow UN inspectors at bombed sites: Iran
item.description: Iran said Tuesday that the UN’s nuclear watchdog will not be able to inspect key nuclear sites bombed by the US and Israel last year, as the first round of talks to end the Middle East war wrapped up in Switzerland....

item.title: NHRC law as drafted risks deepening impunity
item.description: THE National Human Rights Commission has never been able to play the expected role of an independent agency that promotes and protects human rights by acting as an ombudsman. The hope of a reconstituted commission with statutory powers and independence to investigate violations and monitor rights situation is becoming faint as the draft of the......

item.title: Safe food supply warrants strong oversight
item.description: A STUDY conducted by a research team at Hajee Mohammad Danesh Science and Technology University in Dinajpur in association with Noakhali Science and Technology University has found serious bacterial contamination in 80 per cent of the food samples examined. The study was conducted in 2024, but the findings were published in a Swiss journal named Frontiers......

item.title: Education for sustainable development
item.description: IN AN age of globalisation, peace and sustainable development have become shared global imperatives requiring coordinated action, sustained commitment and collective responsibility. A peaceful and sustainable future depends not only on economic growth or technological advancement but on the cultivation of societies that respect cultural diversity, uphold human......

item.title: Limits of current model
item.description: FOR more than three decades, Bangladesh’s economic story has been stitched together by the ready-made garment industry. The steady rhythm of factory floors has powered export growth, generated foreign exchange and, most importantly, provided livelihoods for millions — most of them women — who moved out of poverty through industrial work......

item.title: Dr Yunus’s letter and my search for different path
item.description: THANK you for your letter. I appreciate that you took the time to read my article and respond. Public dialogue is rare, and thoughtful disagreement is rarer still......

item.title: Nine months of DUCSU
item.description: WHEN the Oikkoboddho Shikkharthi Jote swept the Dhaka University Central Students’ Union elections on 9 September 2025, it raised hopes among students who had spent years enduring violence, ragging, and the political capture of residential halls under the successive Bangladesh Chhatra League, the student wing of the Bangladesh Awami League. After the......

item.title: Grieving Deschamps to miss France's final group game
item.description: France coach Didier Deschamps is to return home after his mother died and will miss his team's final World Cup Group I match against Norway, the French Football Federation announced Tuesday....

item.title: Two-goal Ronaldo delights in silencing critics after 'attacks'
item.description:
Cristiano Ronaldo declared ‘I'm back’ and said those ‘attacking’ him should never write him off after making World Cup history in a 5-0 hammering of Uzbekistan on Tuesday in Houston.
...

item.title: England left frustrated by Ghana in draw
item.description: England suffered a World Cup setback on Tuesday as Thomas Tuchel's side were held to a frustrating 0-0 draw by Ghana, leaving them with work still do as they bid to progress to the knockout stage as Group L winners....

item.title: US Congress passes symbolic Iran war rebuke to Trump
item.description: The House-passed measure, adopted by the Senate in a 50-48 vote, directs Trump to remove US forces from hostilities with Iran unless Congress explicitly authorised military action.......

item.title: Teacher training key to early childhood dev: Bobby Hajjaj
item.description: State minister for primary and mass education Bobby Hajjaj on Monday stressed the importance of strengthening teacher training and curriculum reform to advance early childhood development in Bangladesh....

item.title: Bogura residents demand historic names for new unions
item.description: Public hearings were held in Bogura on Tuesday to collect opinions on renaming the three newly created unions under Shibganj and Mokamtala upazilas, with most of the residents demanding names that reflected the areas’ history, heritage and geographical identity....

item.title: ULAB forms AI integration committee
item.description: The University of Liberal Arts Bangladesh has formed an AI integration and digital learning subcommittee as part of its efforts to incorporate artificial intelligence into education, research and institutional operations....

item.title: Adulterated lube oil, raw materials seized in Ctg
item.description: A joint operation conducted by the Bangladesh Coast Guard and the National Consumer Rights Protection Department led to the seizure of a large quantity of adulterated lube oil and raw materials used in its production from a factory in Chattogram’s Karnaphuli area on Monday....

item.title: DU Day to be celebrated on July 1
item.description: Dhaka University Day will be celebrated on July 1, marking the commencement of the university’s academic activities on July 1, 1921....

item.title: 'I'm back': Ronaldo creates history as Portugal run riot
item.description:
Cristiano Ronaldo became the first player to score in six World Cup campaigns and silenced his critics after ‘a dark week’ as the Portugal captain struck twice in a 5-0 annihilation of Uzbekistan on Tuesday.
...

item.title: Ronaldo makes history as Portugal run riot
item.description:
Cristiano Ronaldo became the first player to score in six World Cup campaigns and silenced his critics as the Portugal captain struck twice in a 5-0 annihilation of Uzbekistan on Tuesday.
...

item.title: GAU launches 3-day training on capacity building
item.description: Gazipur Agricultural University inaugurated a three-day training programme titled Capacity Building of Faculty Member of Agriculture, GAU at the university on Monday....

item.title: Lack of coordination mars Dhaka mega transport plan
item.description: Representatives of different authorities on Tuesday raised concern about the lack of coordination among the authorities in the revision of the proposed 20-year long transport plan for the Dhaka metropolitan city....

item.title: DU suspends 3 teachers, relieves 2 from academic duties
item.description: The syndicate of Dhaka University on Monday night decided to temporarily suspend three teachers over allegations of their ‘controversial activities’ during the July uprising and for allegedly threatening students....

item.title: 4-yr university term must be completed on time: minister
item.description: Education minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon on Tuesday said that universities must complete their four-year academic programmes timely....
